title: Jesus' Final Week
description: A compact Passion Week journey from Bethany into Jerusalem and toward the cross and tomb.
caution: The final-week route is built from harmonized Gospel narratives and should be treated as a teaching outline, not a day-by-day reconstruction.

stops.name: Bethany
stops.description: A home base in the final week.
stops.notes: Strongly tied to the Gospel passion narrative.

stops.name: Bethphage / Mount of Olives
stops.description: The place of the triumphal entry preparations.
stops.notes: Bethphage is a traditional designation rather than a fixed modern site.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.description: The city center of the Passion Week events.
stops.notes: A secure location for the major narrative events.

stops.name: Upper Room
stops.description: The meal and farewell discourse setting.
stops.notes: The exact room is not known.

stops.name: Gethsemane
stops.description: The place of prayer and arrest.
stops.notes: A well-known and widely recognized site.

stops.name: The Tomb
stops.notes: The precise burial location is not certain.

segments.label: Entry preparations
segments.caution: The precise route and staging are harmonized.

segments.label: Triumphal entry
segments.caution: A celebratory route is compressed to a single path.

segments.label: Passover meal
segments.caution: The room is traditional rather than certain.

segments.label: Night prayer
segments.caution: The exact path is not detailed.

segments.label: Arrest to cross
segments.caution: A single line represents multiple legal and ritual steps.

segments.label: Burial and rising
segments.description: The burial and empty tomb narrative.
segments.caution: The burial and resurrection sites are traditionally connected.
